project.name: ruvector slice 9 — Neural Trader
project.description: Neural Trader subsystem of RuVector — five Rust crates (coherence, core, replay, strategies, wasm) implementing self-learning, vector-memory-driven trading strategies with a WASM bridge.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the L5 coherence-gate crate; declares the neural-trader-coherence library and pulls in serde/anyhow for the threshold-gate evaluator.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: L5 coherence layer of the Neural Trader: implements the regime-adaptive ThresholdGate with MinCut floors, CUSUM drift detection, boundary-stability gating, and the VerifiedToken / WitnessReceipt audit primitives that the proof-gated mutation protocol relies on.
nodes.languageNotes: Trait-based design with a single default ThresholdGate impl; gate emits four boolean permissions (retrieve/write/learn/act) with regime-conditional floors.

nodes.name: CoherenceDecision
nodes.summary: Output struct of every gate evaluation; carries the four permission booleans plus diagnostic mincut value, partition hash, drift/CUSUM scores, and human-readable reasons.

nodes.name: GateContext
nodes.summary: Input context feeding the coherence gate: symbol/venue/timestamp, current mincut and partition hash, CUSUM and drift scores, regime label, and consecutive stable-boundary window count.

nodes.name: RegimeLabel
nodes.summary: Coarse market-regime enum (Calm / Normal / Volatile) used to pick which MinCut floor the gate enforces.

nodes.name: GateConfig
nodes.summary: Configurable thresholds for the coherence gate: per-regime MinCut floors (12/9/6), CUSUM threshold (4.5), boundary-stability windows (8), and max drift score (0.5).

nodes.name: ThresholdGate
nodes.summary: Default CoherenceGate implementation; evaluates MinCut, CUSUM, drift, and boundary stability against GateConfig and returns a CoherenceDecision with learn requiring a stricter (half) drift margin than act/write.

nodes.name: CoherenceGate
nodes.summary: Single-method trait (`evaluate`) that every coherence gate implementation satisfies, enabling alternative gates (e.g. ML-based) to swap in for ThresholdGate.

nodes.name: VerifiedToken
nodes.summary: Mutation token minted only when the coherence gate and the policy kernel both approve an action; carries hashes that bind the decision to a specific action_intent.

nodes.name: WitnessReceipt
nodes.summary: Immutable audit record appended after every state mutation, hashing the input segment, coherence witness, policy, action intent, verified token, and resulting state.

nodes.name: WitnessLogger
nodes.summary: Trait for appending WitnessReceipt audit records; implemented downstream by neural-trader-replay's InMemoryReceiptLog and any persistent log.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the L1 ingest crate; declares neural-trader-core and pulls in serde/anyhow that the canonical event and graph types depend on.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Top-level Neural Trader architecture document: 6-layer pipeline (L1 Ingest -> L2 Graph -> L3 GNN+Attention -> L4 Memory -> L5 Coherence -> L6 Policy), per-crate type tables, gate semantics, and references to ADR-085/086.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: L1 ingest layer of the Neural Trader: canonical MarketEvent envelope (fixed-point price/qty, venue/symbol ids, 16-byte hashes), 10 NodeKinds, 12 EdgeKinds, 17 PropertyKeys, GraphDelta, plus EventIngestor/GraphUpdater/Embedder traits.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses #[repr(u8)] on every enum to keep the hot ingest path stack-allocated and serde-portable for replay segments.

nodes.name: MarketEvent
nodes.summary: Normalized market event envelope every feed message is converted into before entering the graph or embedding pipeline; carries exchange/ingest timestamps, fixed-point price/qty, venue/symbol ids, and opaque 16-byte hashes for order/participant identity.

nodes.name: EventType
nodes.summary: Seven-variant discriminant for market events: NewOrder, ModifyOrder, CancelOrder, Trade, BookSnapshot, SessionMarker, VenueStatus.

nodes.name: Side
nodes.summary: Order side enum (Bid / Ask) attached to a MarketEvent when applicable.

nodes.name: NodeKind
nodes.summary: Ten-variant enum naming the typed nodes of the heterogeneous market graph (Symbol, Venue, PriceLevel, Order, Trade, Event, Participant, TimeBucket, Regime, StrategyState).

nodes.name: EdgeKind
nodes.summary: Twelve-variant enum for typed edges in the market graph (AtLevel, NextTick, Generated, Matched, ModifiedFrom, CanceledBy, BelongsToSymbol, OnVenue, InWindow, CorrelatedWith, InRegime, AffectsState).

nodes.name: PropertyKey
nodes.summary: Seventeen-variant property-key enum covering depth, queue, imbalance, hazards, slippage, influence, and coherence-contribution metrics — kept as an enum to avoid heap-allocating strings on the hot ingest path.

nodes.name: GraphDelta
nodes.summary: Diff struct returned by GraphUpdater::apply_event listing added nodes, added edges, and property updates produced from a single MarketEvent.

nodes.name: StateWindow
nodes.summary: Sliding window of MarketEvents (symbol, venue, [start_ns, end_ns]) passed to Embedder::embed_state to produce a feature vector for downstream layers.

nodes.name: EventIngestor
nodes.summary: Trait implemented by sinks that accept normalized MarketEvents (e.g. graph projectors, replay writers).

nodes.name: GraphUpdater
nodes.summary: Trait for components that project incoming MarketEvents onto the dynamic heterogeneous graph and emit a GraphDelta.

nodes.name: Embedder
nodes.summary: Trait for components that turn a StateWindow into a Vec<f32> embedding consumed by the L3 GNN+Attention stage.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the L4 replay-memory crate; declares neural-trader-replay and depends on neural-trader-core, neural-trader-coherence, serde, and anyhow.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: L4 replay-memory layer of the Neural Trader: ReplaySegment (signed window of events + embedding + labels + coherence stats + lineage + witness hash), MemoryStore trait, ReservoirStore with gate-guarded O(1) eviction, and InMemoryReceiptLog implementing WitnessLogger.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses VecDeque for O(1) front eviction instead of Vec::remove(0); MemoryStore::maybe_write fails closed when the coherence gate denies write.

nodes.name: ReplaySegment
nodes.summary: Sealed replay window containing serialized events, an optional embedding snapshot, realized labels, coherence statistics, lineage metadata, and a 16-byte witness hash for tamper detection.

nodes.name: SegmentKind
nodes.summary: Seven-variant classification of why a segment was retained (HighUncertainty, LargeImpact, RegimeTransition, StructuralAnomaly, RareQueuePattern, HeadDisagreement, Routine).

nodes.name: CoherenceStats
nodes.summary: Snapshot of coherence diagnostics (mincut, partition hash, drift, CUSUM) captured at the moment a segment was written so audits can reconstruct why memory was admitted.

nodes.name: SegmentLineage
nodes.summary: Lineage metadata (model_id, policy_version, ingest_batch_id) linking a segment back to the model and policy that produced it.

nodes.name: MemoryQuery
nodes.summary: Retrieval query carrying symbol_id, an embedding vector, an optional regime filter, and a result limit.

nodes.name: MemoryStore
nodes.summary: Two-method trait (`retrieve`, `maybe_write`) for selective bounded memory; writes accept a CoherenceDecision so the store can fail closed when the gate denies admission.

nodes.name: InMemoryReceiptLog
nodes.summary: Simple in-memory implementation of WitnessLogger used in tests and research; stores receipts in a Vec and exposes len/is_empty helpers.

nodes.name: ReservoirStore
nodes.summary: Bounded reservoir MemoryStore implementation backed by a VecDeque<ReplaySegment>; honours the coherence gate (returns false when allow_write is false), evicts via O(1) pop_front when full, and retrieves the most recent matching segments by symbol.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the L6 venue-agnostic strategy crate; declares neural-trader-strategies, ties together neural-trader-core / coherence / ruvector-attention dependencies, and configures benchmarks/tests for the EV-Kelly, AttentionScalper, CoherenceArb strategies and the RiskGate.

nodes.name: attention_scalper.rs
nodes.summary: Multi-level order-book imbalance scalper that consumes MarketEvent::BookSnapshot and emits buy/sell Intents; supports two imbalance modes (geometric decay, default; or ruvector-attention ScaledDotProductAttention via use_sdpa) and applies EMA smoothing before crossing the abs_threshold.
nodes.languageNotes: Honest ruvector-attention integration: when use_sdpa is true, each price level is encoded as a [size_log, side_sign, depth_idx, 1.0] vector and the attention-weighted context's sign dimension becomes the signed imbalance.

nodes.name: AttentionScalperConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the scalper: top-N depth, geometric level_decay, ema_alpha smoothing, abs_threshold, contract quantity, strategy_name, and the use_sdpa toggle that switches to ruvector ScaledDotProductAttention.

nodes.name: AttentionScalper
nodes.summary: Stateful per-symbol scalper that maintains yes/no level books, recomputes the imbalance (geometric or SDPA) on every BookSnapshot, smooths via EMA, and emits a YES or NO Intent when |smoothed_imbalance| crosses abs_threshold.

nodes.name: sdpa_imbalance
nodes.summary: Computes signed book imbalance via ruvector_attention::ScaledDotProductAttention; encodes each level as a 4-d vector, queries with a fixed pressure vector, and returns the sign dimension of the attention-weighted context.

nodes.name: update_snapshot
nodes.summary: Ingests one BookSnapshot, appends the level to yes/no buffers, truncates each side to the configured depth, computes raw imbalance (SDPA or geometric), and EMA-smooths it into per-symbol state.

nodes.name: maybe_emit
nodes.summary: Throttled emitter: if the EMA-smoothed imbalance magnitude crosses abs_threshold and the event seq has advanced, returns a Buy YES or Buy NO Intent sized by config.quantity with edge_bps proportional to the imbalance.

nodes.name: coherence_arb.rs
nodes.summary: Cross-market coherence arbitrage strategy: watches operator-configured (reference, mirror) Kalshi pairs and emits a Buy YES on the cheaper side when its mid diverges from the reference by more than min_divergence_bps.

nodes.name: CoherenceArbConfig
nodes.summary: Operator-supplied (reference, mirror) symbol pairs, divergence threshold in basis points, fractional Kelly, bankroll, and strategy attribution name.

nodes.name: CoherenceArb
nodes.summary: Stateful arbitrage strategy that maintains the latest YES-mid for every configured symbol and emits Buy-YES Intents on the divergent leg when prices fall out of coherence.

nodes.name: try_arb_for
nodes.summary: For a given symbol whose mid just updated, walks the configured pair list and returns a Buy-YES Intent on whichever leg now trades more than min_divergence_bps below its partner.

nodes.name: coherence_bridge.rs
nodes.summary: Thin bridge that wires neural-trader-coherence's CoherenceGate into the Strategy -> Intent -> RiskGate -> Order pipeline; re-exports the gate types and exposes a CoherenceChecker that fails closed when the gate errors so internal failures never authorize actuation.

nodes.name: CoherenceChecker
nodes.summary: Pre-order coherence wrapper around any CoherenceGate impl; check() returns Pass(intent) only when allow_act is true, otherwise Block with the decision (and any internal error is folded into a defensive Block).

nodes.name: CoherenceOutcome
nodes.summary: Two-variant enum returned by CoherenceChecker::check: Pass(Intent) or Block{intent, decision}.

nodes.name: simple_context
nodes.summary: Helper that builds a GateContext with sensible defaults for a single intent so callers without a full graph projection can still exercise the gate in paper-trading flows.

nodes.name: ev_kelly.rs
nodes.summary: Expected-Value Kelly strategy for binary Kalshi contracts: external probability prior `p` against market mid `m` cents drives `kelly = (p - m/100) / (1 - m/100)`, sized by a conservative kelly_fraction (default 0.25) and the intent's confidence, then converted into contract count at the current ask.

nodes.name: ExpectedValueKellyConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration carrying kelly_fraction, bankroll_cents, min_edge_bps (defensive pre-RiskGate filter), and attribution name.

nodes.name: ExpectedValueKelly
nodes.summary: Stateful per-symbol EV-Kelly strategy: stores externally supplied priors (clamped to [0.01, 0.99]) and the latest mid, emits at most one Intent per event kind via maybe_emit when the computed edge clears min_edge_bps.

nodes.name: maybe_emit
nodes.summary: Computes Kelly fraction from prior vs mid, applies kelly_fraction and confidence, converts the resulting bankroll fraction into a contract count, and emits a Buy YES or Buy NO Intent if edge_bps clears the configured floor.

nodes.name: intent.rs
nodes.summary: Canonical venue-agnostic Intent struct and its Action / Side enums; this is the type every strategy emits and every downstream stage (CoherenceChecker, RiskGate, venue adapter) consumes.

nodes.name: Action
nodes.summary: Two-variant enum (Buy / Sell) capturing whether an Intent opens or closes a position.

nodes.name: Side
nodes.summary: Binary contract side enum (Yes / No) — distinct from neural_trader_core::Side which is Bid/Ask for limit orders.

nodes.name: Intent
nodes.summary: Strategy intent struct: canonical symbol_id, Yes/No side, Buy/Sell action, limit price in Kalshi cents, quantity, edge in basis points, model confidence in [0,1], and a static strategy attribution string.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Crate root for the L6 strategy runtime: declares the six submodules (attention_scalper, coherence_arb, coherence_bridge, ev_kelly, intent, risk), re-exports the public surface, and defines the venue-agnostic `Strategy` trait that all concrete strategies implement.

nodes.name: Strategy
nodes.summary: Two-method strategy trait: name() for attribution and on_event(&mut self, &MarketEvent) -> Option<Intent>; implementers return at most one Intent per event so composites can stream follow-ups.

nodes.name: risk.rs
nodes.summary: Mandatory RiskGate sitting between every strategy Intent and the venue adapter: enforces non-positive quantity, price-range, daily-loss kill (3% default), edge threshold (300 bps default), hard cash check, single-position cap (10%), cluster concentration (40%), and the KALSHI_ENABLE_LIVE env flag before approving an order.
nodes.languageNotes: Cheapest checks first; failures return RejectReason variants distinguishing InsufficientCash from PositionTooLarge so operators can debug rejections.

nodes.name: Position
nodes.summary: Open-position record (symbol_id, side, quantity, avg_price_cents) with a notional_cents() helper used by cluster accounting.

nodes.name: PortfolioState
nodes.summary: Snapshot of cash, starting cash, day P&L, open positions and symbol->cluster map fed into RiskGate::evaluate; exposes total_notional_cents and cluster_notional_cents helpers.

nodes.name: RiskConfig
nodes.summary: Hard risk limits: max_position_frac (0.10), max_daily_loss_frac (0.03), min_edge_bps (300), max_cluster_frac (0.40), and require_live_flag toggle gating real-money orders behind KALSHI_ENABLE_LIVE=1.

nodes.name: RiskDecision
nodes.summary: Two-variant outcome enum (Approve(Intent) / Reject{reason, intent}) returned by RiskGate::evaluate.

nodes.name: RejectReason
nodes.summary: Eight-variant rejection-reason enum: EdgeTooThin, PositionTooLarge, DailyLossKill, ClusterConcentration, LiveTradingDisabled, InsufficientCash, NonPositiveQuantity, PriceOutOfRange.

nodes.name: RiskGate
nodes.summary: Stateless RiskGate that runs the seven check tiers in order against a PortfolioState and approves or rejects each Intent; closing sells are always allowed so positions can be exited after the daily-loss kill trips.

nodes.name: evaluate
nodes.summary: Core gate routine: runs sanity, daily-loss kill, edge floor, cash check, single-position cap, cluster concentration, and live-flag check in cheapest-first order and returns Approve or a specific RejectReason.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Cargo manifest for the WASM binding crate; configures cdylib output, wasm-bindgen/serde-wasm-bindgen/console_error_panic_hook, and depends on neural-trader-core / coherence / replay for the bindings exposed to @ruvector/neural-trader-wasm.

nodes.name: Dockerfile.test
nodes.summary: Two-stage Docker test runner: stage 1 builds the WASM module with rust:1.83 + wasm-pack against a minimal generated workspace Cargo.toml (only the four neural-trader crates), stage 2 runs the Node smoke test in node:20-slim to verify the WASM module loads and exports are correct.
nodes.languageNotes: Generates a minimal workspace Cargo.toml inline to avoid pulling all 87 RuVector workspace members into the build.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: WASM bindings (ADR-085/ADR-086) exposing the Neural Trader's L1/L4/L5 surface to JavaScript: 7 enum wrappers, MarketEventWasm/GraphDeltaWasm/GateConfigWasm/GateContextWasm/ThresholdGateWasm/CoherenceDecisionWasm/ReplaySegmentWasm/ReservoirStoreWasm wrapper classes, hex helpers for 16-byte hashes, and serde-wasm-bindgen serialization that preserves u64 precision via BigInt.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an enum_convert! macro to bridge wasm-bindgen-compatible enums with native crate enums; serializer is configured with serialize_large_number_types_as_bigints(true) to avoid u64 precision loss in JS.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: WASM start hook that installs console_error_panic_hook when the optional feature is enabled so Rust panics surface as readable JS errors.

nodes.name: hex_to_bytes16_inner
nodes.summary: Parses a 32-char (optional 0x-prefixed) hex string into [u8; 16]; used to round-trip event/order/participant 16-byte hashes across the WASM boundary.

nodes.name: MarketEventWasm
nodes.summary: wasm-bindgen wrapper around neural_trader_core::MarketEvent: exposes getter/setter properties for every field, hex round-trip for the 16-byte hashes, and to_json/from_json helpers.

nodes.name: GraphDeltaWasm
nodes.summary: wasm-bindgen wrapper around neural_trader_core::GraphDelta exposing nodes_added / edges_added / properties_updated as JsValue arrays.

nodes.name: GateConfigWasm
nodes.summary: WASM wrapper around neural_trader_coherence::GateConfig with getter/setter pairs for every threshold so JavaScript can configure the regime-adaptive coherence gate.

nodes.name: GateContextWasm
nodes.summary: WASM wrapper around neural_trader_coherence::GateContext; constructor accepts the partition_hash as a hex string and the regime as a RegimeLabelWasm enum, with to_inner() to materialize the native GateContext on demand.

nodes.name: ThresholdGateWasm
nodes.summary: WASM wrapper around neural_trader_coherence::ThresholdGate; `evaluate` consumes a GateContextWasm and returns a CoherenceDecisionWasm so JS callers can drive the gate end-to-end.

nodes.name: CoherenceDecisionWasm
nodes.summary: WASM wrapper around CoherenceDecision exposing the four permission booleans, diagnostic values, all_allowed/fully_blocked convenience flags, and to_json for JS-side logging.

nodes.name: ReplaySegmentWasm
nodes.summary: WASM wrapper around neural_trader_replay::ReplaySegment exposing the core ids, time range, and to_json/from_json round-trip for JS callers.

nodes.name: ReservoirStoreWasm
nodes.summary: WASM wrapper around neural_trader_replay::ReservoirStore; exposes new/len/is_empty plus maybe_write (gate-aware) and retrieve_by_symbol so JS callers can drive the bounded gate-guarded memory store from the browser.

nodes.name: node-smoke.mjs
nodes.summary: Node 20 smoke test that loads the wasm-pack-built @ruvector/neural-trader-wasm module and asserts every public export (init, version, healthCheck, MarketEventWasm round-trip, GateConfigWasm, ThresholdGateWasm gate evaluation, ReservoirStoreWasm) behaves as the Rust crate documents.

layers.name: Trader Core (L1 Ingest + L4 Memory)
layers.description: Canonical MarketEvent envelope, GraphDelta types, EventIngestor/GraphUpdater/Embedder traits (L1), plus the ReplaySegment / ReservoirStore / InMemoryReceiptLog memory layer (L4) that records the witness-hashed event windows feeding every downstream stage.

layers.name: Coherence Gate (L5)
layers.description: L5 ThresholdGate with MinCut floors, CUSUM drift detection, boundary-stability gating, plus the VerifiedToken and WitnessReceipt audit primitives that implement the proof-gated mutation protocol referenced by replay, strategies, and the WASM bindings.

layers.name: Strategy Runtime (L6 Policy + Risk)
layers.description: Venue-agnostic Strategy trait, the shared Intent type, four concrete strategies (AttentionScalper, CoherenceArb, EV-Kelly, CoherenceBridge to the gate), and the mandatory RiskGate enforcing kill-switch, edge, single-position, and cluster-concentration policy before any venue actuation.

layers.name: WASM JavaScript Bindings
layers.description: ADR-085/086 WASM surface that exposes L1/L4/L5 types (MarketEventWasm, GraphDeltaWasm, ThresholdGateWasm, ReservoirStoreWasm) to JavaScript via wasm-bindgen with BigInt-preserving u64 serialization, plus the Node 20 smoke test that exercises every public export.

layers.name: Manifests, Docs, and Test Infrastructure
layers.description: Per-crate Cargo manifests wiring up the four neural-trader libraries and the WASM cdylib, the top-level Neural Trader architecture README documenting the L1-L6 pipeline, and the two-stage Dockerfile.test that builds the WASM module with wasm-pack and runs the Node smoke test.

tour.title: Slice 9 Architecture Overview
tour.description: Start with the Neural Trader README, which lays out the 6-layer pipeline (L1 Ingest -> L2 Graph -> L3 GNN+Attention -> L4 Memory -> L5 Coherence -> L6 Policy), the per-crate type tables, gate semantics, and the ADR-085/086 references that drive Slice 9's proof-gated mutation protocol. This is the roadmap every subsequent step builds on.

tour.title: Slice 9 L1 Ingest - Core Types
tour.description: neural-trader-core defines the canonical vocabulary the rest of the pipeline speaks: the MarketEvent envelope with fixed-point price/qty and 16-byte hashes, the 10 NodeKinds / 12 EdgeKinds / 17 PropertyKeys that describe the graph (L2), GraphDelta updates, and the EventIngestor/GraphUpdater/Embedder traits that downstream layers plug into. Every later step refers back to these types.
tour.languageLesson: Rust newtype + fixed-point encoding: representing price/qty as integers (e.g. i64 scaled by 10^k) instead of f64 eliminates floating-point non-determinism, which is essential for deterministic replay and cryptographic hashing of MarketEvents.

tour.title: Slice 9 L5 Coherence Gate
tour.description: neural-trader-coherence implements the regime-adaptive ThresholdGate that decides whether an action is allowed to mutate state. It combines MinCut floors, CUSUM drift detection, and boundary-stability checks, and emits VerifiedToken / WitnessReceipt audit primitives that the proof-gated mutation protocol from Step 1 relies on. This is the single chokepoint every later strategy must clear.

tour.title: Slice 9 L4 Memory - Replay Reservoir
tour.description: neural-trader-replay is the time-series memory of the trader. ReplaySegment captures a signed window of MarketEvents (from Step 2) plus its embedding, labels, coherence stats, lineage and witness hash; ReservoirStore provides O(1) gate-guarded eviction; and InMemoryReceiptLog implements WitnessLogger so every stored segment is auditable against the L5 gate from Step 3.
tour.languageLesson: Trait-bounded generics in Rust let neural-trader-replay define the MemoryStore trait once and then provide a concrete ReservoirStore implementation; downstream layers can swap in a disk-backed store without changing call sites, which keeps the proof-gated invariants stable across implementations.

tour.title: Slice 9 L6 Policy - Intent + Strategy Trait
tour.description: neural-trader-strategies is the policy layer. lib.rs declares the venue-agnostic Strategy trait and wires up the six submodules; intent.rs defines the canonical Intent / Action / Side types that every strategy emits and every downstream stage (CoherenceChecker, RiskGate, venue adapter) consumes. intent.rs has the highest fan-in in the whole slice - it is the contract that holds L6 together.

tour.title: Slice 9 Strategies + Coherence Bridge
tour.description: The four concrete strategies all emit Intents from Step 5 but reach the market through different signals: attention_scalper consumes BookSnapshots and applies geometric or ScaledDotProductAttention imbalance with EMA smoothing; coherence_arb watches Kalshi reference/mirror pairs for divergence; ev_kelly sizes binary Kalshi positions via fractional Kelly against a prior; and coherence_bridge wires the L5 CoherenceGate (Step 3) into the pipeline as a fail-closed CoherenceChecker so internal errors never authorize actuation.

tour.title: Slice 9 RiskGate - Last Line Before the Venue
tour.description: risk.rs is the mandatory RiskGate that sits between every Intent (Step 5) and the venue adapter. It enforces non-positive quantity, price-range, a 3% daily-loss kill, 300 bps edge threshold, hard cash check, 10% single-position cap, 40% cluster concentration, and the KALSHI_ENABLE_LIVE env flag before any order is approved. Combined with the L5 coherence gate from Step 3, this gives Slice 9 its two-stage proof-gated execution guarantee.

tour.title: Slice 9 WASM Surface + Containerized Smoke Test
tour.description: neural-trader-wasm (per ADR-085/086) exposes the L1/L4/L5 surface to JavaScript via wrapper classes (MarketEventWasm, ThresholdGateWasm, ReservoirStoreWasm) with hex helpers and serde-wasm-bindgen that preserves u64 via BigInt. node-smoke.mjs round-trips every export, and Dockerfile.test runs the whole thing in a two-stage rust:1.83 -> node:20-slim container so the WASM module is verified the same way in CI as on a developer laptop.
tour.languageLesson: wasm-bindgen cannot pass u64 across the JS boundary as a JS Number (which is f64 and loses precision above 2^53); the workaround used here is serde-wasm-bindgen with the BigInt option, which materializes u64 fields as BigInt on the JS side - critical for the 16-byte hash and nanosecond-timestamp fields the L1 envelope carries.
